items.definition_en: A savings account is a bank account where you keep money to save.
items.example_en: She put her birthday money in her savings account.

items.definition_en: A deposit is money you put into a bank or savings account.
items.example_en: He made a deposit at the bank yesterday.

items.definition_en: A withdrawal is money you take out from your bank account.
items.example_en: She made a withdrawal from the ATM.

items.definition_en: A balance is the amount of money you have in your account.
items.example_en: He checked his balance online.

items.definition_en: An investment is something you put money into to try to make more money.
items.example_en: Buying a house can be a good investment.

items.definition_en: A bond is a way of saving money by lending it to the government or a company.
items.example_en: My grandparents bought a bond for me.

items.definition_en: A stock is a type of investment in a company.
items.example_en: He bought stock in a technology company.

items.definition_en: A share is a part of ownership in a company.
items.example_en: She owns a share in the business.

items.definition_en: A fund is a sum of money saved or gathered for a special purpose.
items.example_en: They started a fund for the new playground.

items.definition_en: A piggy bank is a container used to save small amounts of money.
items.example_en: The boy put coins in his piggy bank every week.

items.definition_en: An account is a place at a bank where you keep your money.
items.example_en: My parents opened an account for me.

items.definition_en: A cashbox is a small box where cash is kept.
items.example_en: She placed the day's savings in the cashbox.

items.definition_en: A treasury is a place or fund where money is kept or managed.
items.example_en: The country's gold is stored in the treasury.

items.definition_en: A deposit slip is a form you fill in when putting money into a bank.
items.example_en: He filled out a deposit slip at the bank.

items.definition_en: A retirement fund is money saved for when you stop working.
items.example_en: She adds money to her retirement fund every month.

items.definition_en: A safe is a strong box used to keep money and valuables secure.
items.example_en: They locked their savings in a safe.

items.definition_en: A saver is a person who keeps money instead of spending it.
items.example_en: He became a good saver from a young age.

items.definition_en: An allowance is money given regularly to save or spend.
items.example_en: His parents give him a weekly allowance.

items.definition_en: A nest egg is money saved for the future.
items.example_en: She has a nest egg for emergencies.

items.definition_en: A goal is something you hope to save money for.
items.example_en: His goal is to save money for a new bike.
